_meta.purpose: Forward-looking capital market assumptions for the ETF-investable asset classes a Singapore retail investor can access. Synthesised house estimates, informed by published long-run (10-15yr) assumptions from major asset managers (2025-26 vintage). NOT a reproduction of any single provider's proprietary table. NOT a forecast. Educational use only.
_meta.return_basis: Geometric, per annum. USD-basis except classes marked basis=SGD. SGD-based investors face an additional FX consideration on USD-basis classes (~ +/-1% p.a. unhedged, or the hedging cost) — surfaced in-app, not baked into these numbers.
_meta.risk_free_note: USD cash anchor used for Sharpe across all classes for consistency. Verified points: JPM 2026 LTCMA USD cash expected return 3.1% (its cycle-neutral yield is 2.9%), BlackRock 3.6%, Vanguard range 2.9-3.9%. We keep the anchor at 2.9 — the conservative end of the verified set (Vanguard's low end, coinciding with JPM's cycle-neutral yield), not any provider's expected-return point. A lower anchor flatters every Sharpe slightly; re-anchoring on JPM's 3.1 would reduce each class's Sharpe by 0.2/vol. SGD cash ~1.8% long-run (structurally below USD; MAS runs exchange-rate policy). Sharpe = (ret - risk_free) / vol.
_meta.confidence: Round-number estimates. Treat as directional, not precise. Wide bands apply. These are LONG-RUN AVERAGES over the horizon, not today's rates — short-rate classes (cash, SGD bonds) in particular will differ from current board/promotional rates in either direction.
_meta.verified: 2026-07-09 — three-agent web verification against: JPM 2026 LTCMA (press release + executive summary, Oct 2025), BlackRock CMA data workbook (May 2026, data 31 Mar 2026), Vanguard VCMM 10yr ranges (31 Mar 2026 run), WGC GLTER (Oct 2024), OCBC S-REIT research (Mar 2026), and Jul-2026 market/factsheet yields (SORA, SGS, A35, MBH, ES3, KHYB). Per-class sources in each informed_by.
_meta.revision_log: 2026-07-09 pass 1 (consistency): cash 3.0->2.2, sgd_bonds 3.2->3.0. 2026-07-09 pass 2 (verified, sources in informed_by): cash 2.2->1.8 and sgd_bonds 3.0->2.5 — retrieved starting yields sit far below the earlier figures (3M SORA 1.12%, A35 YTM 2.08%, MBH YTM 2.74%, 10Y SGS 2.14%); dev_bonds 4.4->4.5 (on the BlackRock global-agg-hedged point); risk_free 2.8->2.9 (JPM USD cash); sg_equity building block corrected to the actual ~3.2% STI yield (number kept); dev_equity, em_asia, thematic, reits, gold, asia_bonds verified and kept. 2026-07-24 pass 3 (attribution, cross-checked against JPM 2026 fixed-income Exhibit 4A via the KAsset KCMA 2026 reprint and the Long-Term-Portfolio-Construction README Guard 1 reconciliation): risk_free KEPT at 2.9 but re-labelled — 2.9 is JPM's cycle-neutral cash yield and Vanguard's low end, NOT JPM's expected return, which is 3.1; the pass-2 rationale '(JPM USD cash)' carried the wrong label on the right number. dev_bonds informed_by re-pointed to reproducible figures (JPM US aggregate 4.8, Treasuries 4.0 intermediate / 4.6 10-year — the earlier 4.9 endpoint could not be reproduced from either column of the exhibit); cash informed_by USD range restated as 2.9-3.9 with named points. US HY 6.1 and US IG 5.2 confirmed as expected returns, not yields. No numeric field changed; Sharpe and Efficiency Score outputs are identical.

dev_equity.label: Global / Developed Equity
dev_equity.role: Core growth engine (world & US developed markets).
dev_equity.informed_by: Verified points (10yr+, USD): JPM 2026 global equity 7.0% (US large cap 6.7%, EAFE 7.5%); BlackRock World 8.3%; Vanguard ranges US 4.9-6.9% / DM ex-US 5.0-7.0%. A wide spread — we sit near the JPM point, below BlackRock, above Vanguard.

sg_equity.label: Singapore Equity
sg_equity.informed_by: STI dividend yield ~3.2% (ES3, Jul 2026 — compressed by the 2025-26 rally) plus ~3-3.5% long-run nominal earnings and dividend growth; SGD basis.

em_asia_equity.label: EM & Asia ex-Japan Equity
em_asia_equity.role: Higher-growth diversifier; higher volatility, cheaper valuations.
em_asia_equity.informed_by: Verified points: JPM 2026 EM 7.8%; BlackRock EM 7.3%; Vanguard EM 3.6-5.6% — the widest provider disagreement of any class. We sit with the JPM/BlackRock pair.

thematic_equity.label: Thematic / Sector Equity
thematic_equity.role: Concentrated sector/theme bets (tech, EV, low-carbon). No return premium assumed over broad equity; much higher idiosyncratic risk. Satellite only.
thematic_equity.informed_by: Broad equity return with a large volatility uplift for concentration.

dev_bonds.label: Global / US Aggregate Bonds
dev_bonds.role: Core ballast; flight-to-quality in equity sell-offs.
dev_bonds.informed_by: Starting yields (verified): BlackRock global aggregate (hedged) 4.5% / US aggregate 4.8%; Vanguard US agg 4.2-5.2%; JPM US aggregate 4.8% and Treasuries 4.0% (intermediate) to 4.6% (10-year) bracket the figure.

sgd_bonds.label: SGD Bonds (SG Govt / IG)
sgd_bonds.role: SGD-denominated ballast; no currency risk for a SG investor.
sgd_bonds.informed_by: Starting yields, 31 May 2026 factsheets: A35 (SGS, AAA) YTM 2.08%, MBH (SGD IG corp) YTM 2.74%, 10Y SGS 2.14% — a govt/IG blend lands at 2.2-2.6%; ~70bp term/credit premium over long-run SGD cash.

asia_bonds.label: Asia / EM Bonds
asia_bonds.role: Higher-yield credit; note higher equity correlation than govt bonds.
asia_bonds.informed_by: Current Asia USD credit yields ~6.1-6.6% (KHYB SEC yield 6.12%, Jul 2026; O9P weighted coupon 6.64%); JPM 2026 US HY 6.1%, US IG 5.2%. A regional govt/IG/HY blend nets slightly below the HY points.

reits.label: REITs (SG / Asia)
reits.role: Listed real-estate income; inflation-sensitive, equity-like in stress.
reits.informed_by: S-REIT forward distribution yield ~6.2% (OCBC, Mar 2026; sector range 5.9-6.3%) plus modest DPU growth net of dilution (OCBC forecasts +0.5%/+3.0% FY1/FY2). Global listed-REIT CMAs bracket us: BlackRock 6.6%, Northern Trust 6.2%, State Street 6.1%.

gold.label: Gold
gold.role: Tail-risk hedge; near-zero equity correlation; central-bank demand.
gold.informed_by: Verified anchors: JPM 2026 LTCMA gold 5.5% (raised from 4.5%); World Gold Council GLTER 5.2% p.a. (2025-40). We sit slightly below both — only these two institutions publish a long-run gold figure; BlackRock/State Street/Northern Trust do not.

cash.label: Cash (SGD)
cash.role: Liquidity buffer; opportunity cost in bull markets.
cash.informed_by: 3M compounded SORA 1.12% (Jul 2026); ~1% average through 2010-20; SGD short rates run structurally below USD because MAS manages the exchange rate, not interest rates (BIS Papers 73). USD cash CMAs 2.9-3.9% (Vanguard range; JPM 3.1 and BlackRock 3.6 points) minus the historical SGD discount lands here; the ~3% of 2024-25 was a cyclical high.

_corr_note: Pairwise correlations, informed by ~20yr exponentially-weighted history from the source SAA work. Symmetric; diagonal = 1; any pair not listed defaults to 0.30 and is reported by the pipeline so nothing is silently assumed. In crisis regimes equity-credit and equity-REIT correlations spike toward 0.85+.

us_estate._note: US estate (inheritance) tax on US-situs assets held by a Singapore-resident non-resident-alien. Situs follows the underlying asset, NOT the broker. Educational; not tax advice.
us_estate.source: Financial Horse, 'Die holding US Stocks and pay 40% tax?' (3 Jul 2026); cross-checked to IRC 2001(c) graduated schedule. Singapore has no US estate-tax treaty.

us_div_withholding._note: Withholding tax suffered on US-source dividends INSIDE the fund, by fund domicile. Irish/Lux UCITS get the 15% treaty rate; US-domiciled funds held by a Singaporean suffer 30%; synthetic (swap-based) funds can reduce this further but are flagged separately. Applied only to the US-dividend portion of a fund's income.
us_div_withholding._content_note: us_content = fraction of the fund's income that is US-source and subject to US DIVIDEND withholding. Estimates. drag = assumed_div_yield * us_content * wht_rate. Bond funds are set near-zero: US-source INTEREST paid to a foreign fund is largely exempt under the US portfolio-interest exemption, so a global aggregate suffers little US withholding despite ~40% US content. Models the dominant US-dividend case only; non-US withholding (e.g. China ~10%) is not modelled and is flagged where material.
